A test case verifying that multiplying false by false results in 0.

# Purpose
This code is a simple assertion test that checks the result of multiplying two boolean `false` values in JavaScript. The expression `false * false` evaluates to `0` because JavaScript converts `false` to `0` when performing arithmetic operations. The `assert` function verifies that the expression evaluates to `0`, and if it does not, the program will throw an error. This code provides narrow functionality, serving as a basic test to confirm the behavior of boolean arithmetic in JavaScript.
